title: Grail investors face August 4 deadline in securities class action
description: Purchasers of Grail, Inc. common stock between May 13, 2025, and February 19, 2026, have until August 4, 2026, to seek appointment as lead plaintiff in a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that defendants made materially false and misleading statements regarding the NHS-Galleri trial, which failed to meet its primary endpoint of statistically significant Stage III-IV cancer reduction. title: 'Stock market looking more like gambling:' Here's a Warren Buffett warning to investors
description: Warren Buffett has cautioned that parts of the stock market increasingly resemble gambling rather than investing, as retail traders, AI-driven optimism and speculative options activity fuel Wall Street's rally. The Berkshire Hathaway chairman urged investors to focus on long-term value instead of chasing quick gains amid rising market exuberance.

title: Rupee hits two-month low, oil prices remain focal point
description: The Indian rupee hit a two-month low on Monday as oil prices hovered around ninety dollars per barrel. In an effort to stabilize the currency, the Reserve Bank of India intervened by unloading dollars into the market. Meanwhile, Asian currencies and stock performances were varied, with the dollar index holding steady. This rise in energy prices could have implications for the Federal Reserve's future interest rate strategies.

title: How rural India is bearing the brunt of the inflation rise
description: India’s rural inflation has moved closer to 5%, while the urban rate remains below 4%. The gap is usually explained by higher food prices, but the latest data suggest price pressures may be spreading beyond food.
content: Rural inflation typically accelerates when food prices are rising or remain elevated. Food and beverages account for 23.2% of the rural basket, compared with 13.5% of the urban basket. With an uneven monsoon and the risk of deficient rainfall raising... [3143 chars]

title: RBI Governor flags Iran war, weak monsoon as the biggest risks to India’s inflation and economic growth
description: Elevated inflation persists due to West Asian tensions and a weak monsoon outlook. The Reserve Bank of India maintains its policy rate, diverging from global peers. Governor Malhotra stated that domestic inflation pass-through has remained limited so far. India's economic fundamentals remain strong despite geopolitical uncertainties and growth forecasts. Policymakers will monitor inflationary pressures before considering any monetary policy adjustments.

title: HSBC offers 19x FCNR(B) leverage in GIFT City
description: HSBC's GIFT City unit offers NRIs significant leverage on FCNR deposits. An NRI can borrow up to nineteen times their own funds for deposits. This special deposit program attracts overseas dollar deposits and boosts inflows. The Reserve Bank of India temporarily relaxed interest rate ceilings for this purpose. This initiative aims to attract foreign exchange until September 2026.

title: Can banks increase FD interest rates as inflation crosses RBI’s target of 4%?
description: Rising inflation has crossed the Reserve Bank of India's target, signaling potential repo rate hikes. Higher inflation and increased credit demand suggest banks might raise fixed deposit interest rates soon. Banks monitor deposit growth and credit demand before adjusting their fixed deposit rates. High yields on government securities and small savings schemes also pressure banks to increase FD rates.

title: India bonds extend recovery tracking rise in U.S. Treasuries
description: Indian government bonds saw gains for a second straight session on Thursday. This followed overnight increases in U.S. Treasuries after softer economic data emerged. Softer U.S. inflation data eased concerns about an imminent Federal Reserve rate hike. Lower U.S. yields eased pressure on emerging-market debt, helping Indian bonds recover. State-run lenders and foreign investors actively bought bonds, adding support.
